title: Decide RepoBrief agent adoption from paired evidence
goal: Review the fixed benchmark evidence, classify useful and harmful task classes, keep RepoBrief opt-in, prefer it for bounded classes, or reject the path, and only then decide whether incremental rebuild or watcher work is justified.

approval.note: Any preference or default-route decision must cite the complete paired benchmark and preserve negative or null findings.

acceptance.assertion: Every task class is classified as reproducibly useful, neutral, harmful or insufficient-evidence using the pre-registered metrics and both benchmark repetitions.
Quality, citation, false-confidence, freshness, invalid-run and safety regressions are listed without averaging them away through token or time savings.
The decision is one of retain-opt-in, prefer-for-explicit-classes, reject, or insufficient-evidence; default promotion remains a separate explicit authorization.
Bureau issue #482 is promoted into active implementation only if the benchmark shows that freshness latency or rebuild cost materially limits an otherwise useful RepoBrief path.
The final report links immutable raw transcripts, requests, receipts, evaluation digests, exact runner/model identity and all invalid or failed runs.

lease_scope_migration.rationale: RepoBrief adoption is decided from Lenskit evidence and recorded as Bureau task and initiative truth.
does_not_establish: safe mutation of terminal historical tasks
